Q2 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For a flip-flop with provisions for preset and clear.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The preset input drives the flip-flop to a set state while the clear input drives it to a reset state. Hence, both are disabled or only one is enabled at a time.

Q3 Not checked

Not attempted

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q4 Not checked

Not attempted

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q5 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The table which shows the necessary levels at J and K inputs to produce every possible flip-flop state transition is called

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The excitation table of the flip-flop shows the required excitation to the flip-flop, or the required input to the flip-flop, to go from the given state to the next particular state.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a 20-bit counter type A/D converter using a 2.5 MHz clock, the minimum conversion rate is ________ conversions/sec
(Rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.38 (2.35 - 2.43)
Solution

2.38 (2.35 - 2.43)

(Rounded off to two decimal places)
NAT

Solution

Q8 Not checked

A 5-bit shift register circuit configured for right-shift operation, i.e. Din → A, A → B, B → C, C → D, D → E is shown. If the present state of the shift register is ABCDE = 10110, the number of clock cycles required to reach the state ABCDE = 11111 is _______.

NAT
Correct answer: 4 (4 - 4)
Solution

4 (4 - 4)
